core_question: Is the Stigmatization Thesis a Valid Linguistic Observation or a Gender-Grievance Narrative?
verdict_summary: Partially valid observation in a poorly executed narrative
consensus_points: Some female-radical characters (嫖妓娼妖) underwent semantic pejoration - this is philologically documented
The phenomenon correlates with gender dynamics but causal attribution remains unproven
The original article contains factual errors, cherry-picking, and overgeneralization
The research direction has legitimate academic value
Etymology education in schools is the most practical and non-controversial action

reliability_ranking.SEAT-1: HIGH - strongest factual foundation, aligned with CLAIM_GATE
reliability_ranking.SEAT-2: HIGH - academic framework, honest about article flaws
reliability_ranking.SEAT-4: HIGH - credible personal testimony, practical recommendations
reliability_ranking.SEAT-6: MEDIUM - well-intentioned but relayed unverified information
reliability_ranking.SEAT-3: VERY LOW - fabricated anecdotes, position inconsistency, emotional manipulation
